_doc.purpose: RCD-type and way-count lookup for UK consumer units. Driven by board manufacturer + model identification from the Stage 1 classifier. The lookup runs BEFORE per-slot waveform reads commit, and a 'high' confidence hit overrides the per-slot VLM (which mis-reads the BS EN 61009 waveform glyph because it's sub-millimetre). Promoted entries from field testers should land here via scripts/promote-rcd-lookup.js.

confidence_levels.high: Override per-slot reads unconditionally. Use only when verified against the manufacturer datasheet AND/OR cross-confirmed by 5+ field extractions.
confidence_levels.medium: Use as default. Per-slot reads at confidence >= 0.95 that disagree are honoured (covers the 'older RCBO retrofitted into newer board' case).
confidence_levels.low: Hint only. Fills nulls but never overrides an existing per-slot read.

verified_by_values.datasheet: Cross-checked against the manufacturer's published datasheet by a human.
verified_by_values.production: Confirmed by 5+ successful field extractions (no inspector corrections).
verified_by_values.field: Confirmed by an inspector after on-device verification.
verified_by_values.literature: Industry-known default; not yet verified for this exact entry. Treat as a starting point.
verified_by_values.pending: Auto-promoted from rcd-lookup-pending without human review (avoid in production).

_doc.manufacturer_key_normalisation: lowercase, alphanumeric only, '_' separator (see normaliseManufacturer in src/extraction/rcd-type-lookup.js).
_doc.model_key_normalisation: uppercase, whitespace stripped, '_' / '-' / digits / letters preserved (see normaliseModel).

matched_via_values.alias: Manufacturer key was resolved through manufacturer_aliases (e.g. 'Click Scolmore' → 'Elucian').
matched_via_values.confusable: Model key didn't match directly but a single-character confusable substitution did (e.g. 'CU1SPD27S' → 'CU1SPD275'). Common Stage 1 OCR errors: S↔5, O↔0, I↔1, B↔8, G↔6, Z↔2.

elucian.note: All current Elucian (Click Scolmore) RCBO consumer units are Type A. Confirmed via production extractions and inspector verification.

wylex.note: Modern NH-series and AMR RCBOs are Type A. Older NSBS retrofits may be Type AC — keep medium so confident per-slot AC reads are honoured.

bg.note: BG Fortress current range (CFUDP/CUCRB RCBOs) is Type A. Verify before promoting to high.

fusebox.note: CPN / Europa Fusebox FB-series RCBOs are Type A across the modern range.

chint.note: Chint NHB1L RCBO range is Type A.

schneider.note: Easy9 (EZ9D) and Resi9 RCBO ranges are Type A. Older Acti9 stock may be Type AC.

mk.note: MK Sentry K/KQ-series RCBOs are Type A. Older Sentry stock may be Type AC.

hager.note: Series-dependent. VML/VYP/VCL Design 10/30 are Type A; older VKM and some VMS variants are Type AC. Prefer model-level lookup; null default forces fallthrough.

eaton.note: Memshield 3 RCBOs vary. Crabtree-branded Starbreaker (now Eaton) is mostly Type AC on older stock, Type A on current. Prefer model-level lookup.

crabtree.note: Mostly migrated to Eaton branding. Starbreaker bank-RCD boards are typically Type AC; newer RCBO modules Type A. Prefer model-level lookup.

contactum.note: Defender (CPCNR) RCBOs are Type A; older Contactum stock varies. Prefer model-level lookup.

proteus.note: Geyer Proteus RCBO range is Type A.

elucian/CU1SPD275.note: Confirmed across multiple field extractions (user 82b54893, May 2026). 15-way DIN-rail with SPD.

_changelog.change: Initial seed. Elucian default + CU1SPD275 promoted to high (production-confirmed). Other UK majors seeded at medium/low for tester rollout — pending field verification via scripts/promote-rcd-lookup.js.
